name: golang-github-gin-contrib-sse-dev
title: golang-github-gin-contrib-sse-dev
description:
Server-Sent Events implementation in Go
Server-sent events (SSE) is a technology where a browser receives
automatic updates from a server via HTTP connection. The Server-Sent
Events EventSource API is standardized as part of HTML5 by the W3C.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-gin-contrib-sse-dev because Server-Sent Events implementation in Go.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-gin-contrib-sse-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-gin-contrib-sse-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-gin-contrib-sse-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
